reasoning: Confirmed verbatim at line 171: wp_enqueue_style() loads '//ajax.googleapis.com/ajax/libs/jqueryui/{$ui->ver}/themes/smoothness/jquery-ui.min.css' with no integrity/SRI argument. The hook is registered unconditionally on admin_enqueue_scripts (line 25) with no post-type guard inside _admin_enqueue_scripts(), so the external resource loads on every admin page for any authenticated admin-panel user. Protocol-relative URL degrades to HTTP on non-HTTPS admin installations. WordPress wp_enqueue_style() supports an $args array for integrity since WP 6.3; it is not used here. Reachability claim ('any admin user loading a mw-wp-form edit page') is actually understated — the resource loads on ALL admin pages, not just form edit pages. Impact is correctly rated low: CSS injection can rewrite admin UI (fake overlays, clickjacking via visibility) but cannot execute JS directly. No sanitizer or guard was missed. Finding is real.
